top_signals.title: Russia Slashes Oil Exports As Fuel Shortages And Drone Attacks Bite
top_signals.summary: Russia is preparing to sharply reduce crude oil exports this month as mounting refinery disruptions, fuel shortages, and Ukraine’s bombing campaign force Moscow to divert more barrels into the domestic market. Exports from Russia’s western ports of Primorsk, Ust-Luga and Novorossiysk are expected to fall to roughly 1.7 million barrels per day in June from 2.5 million bpd in May, according to Reuters calculations based on preliminary industry and trading data. The decline comes as Russia seeks to increase refinery throughput to address…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: US approves Kuwait request to buy nearly $2 billion of counter-drone platforms
top_signals.summary: Following recent Iranian strikes on Kuwait, the U.S. approved a potential foreign military sale of counter-UAS platforms made by Anduril.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: The incredible shrinking influence of Putin’s Russia
top_signals.summary: Armenia is the latest country to leave Moscow’s orbit

top_signals.title: Intesa’s bid for MPS restores some sanity to Italy’s M&A scene
top_signals.summary: An Intesa victory might be appealing to Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni

top_signals.title: Coinbase strategist says institutions aren’t panicking about bitcoin, ‘love it even more’ at lower prices
top_signals.summary: Family offices and sovereign wealth funds continue accumulating bitcoin despite the recent selloff, according to Coinbase's John D'Agostino.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.
